SEARCH
Package com.nib.commons.storage

Class NGroup

java.lang.Object
com.nib.commons.storage.AbstractNField
com.nib.commons.storage.NGroup
All Implemented Interfaces:
IDataArea, Cloneable
Direct Known Subclasses:
CEEDAYS.CeedaysLinkage, CEEDAYS.CeedaysWorking, CEEDAYS.LkDate, CEEDAYS.LkFc, CEEDAYS.LkFc.ConditionTokenValue, CEEDAYS.LkFc.ConditionTokenValue.Case1ConditionId, CEEDAYS.LkFc.ConditionTokenValue.Case2ConditionId, CEEDAYS.LkFormat, Dfheiblk, ExecParm, MessageDescriptor, NVarcharField, Sqlca, StructMqtm, TcpSocketParm, TcpSocketParm.ClientId, TcpSocketParm.SockAddrInParm
public abstract class NGroup extends AbstractNField
The NGroup class is a representation of a storage group that allows for hierarchical data organization and manipulation. It serves as a base for defining specific group structures and provides methods for allocation, memory management, and data manipulation. This class extends the AbstractNField class and includes various operations to handle child elements, manage memory, and perform comparisons or relational operations on group fields.

      Returns:
      the length the field as an integer

    • calculateLength

      public int calculateLength()
      Calculates the total length of the fields within the NGroup instance. The length is determined by iterating over all declared fields in the class, and summing up the lengths of eligible fields based on the following criteria: - If the field is an instance of AbstractNField, its length is included unless it is marked as a "redefinition". - If the field is an instance of IDataArea, its length is included. - If the field is an array of the IDataArea type, the length of each element in the array is included.
      Returns:
      the total calculated length of the fields within the instance

    • dumpStructure

      public void dumpStructure(String name, String indent)
      Description copied from interface: IDataArea
      Dumps the structure of the data area.
      Parameters:
      name - the name of the data area
      indent - the indentation to use for the structure

      public void setValue(int value)

    • setValue

      public void setValue(StringValue value)

    • setValue

      public void setValue(AbstractNElementary field)
      Sets the value of the current instance by transferring the data from the specified AbstractNElementary field and padding any remaining space.
      Parameters:
      field - the AbstractNElementary field whose data will be moved to the current instance. The method aligns the data and pads excess space if needed.

    • setValue

      public void setValue(NGroup field)
      Sets the value of the current instance by transferring the data from the given NGroup field and padding any remaining space as needed.
      Parameters:
      field - the NGroup field whose data will be moved to the current instance. The method aligns the data and fills excess space with blanks if necessary.

    • equals

      public boolean equals(String value)
      Compares the provided string value with the value of the current instance to determine if they are equal. Comparison is case-sensitive and considers null values as not equal.
      Parameters:
      value - the string value to be compared with the current instance. A null value will result in the comparison returning false.
      Returns:
      true if the provided string is equal to the string representation of the current instance; false otherwise.

    • getChildren

      public List<AbstractNField> getChildren()
      Retrieves the list of child fields associated with the current instance.
      Returns:
      a list of child fields represented as instances of AbstractNField.

    • getAllChildren

      public List<AbstractNField> getAllChildren()
      Retrieves a list of all child fields associated with the current instance, including nested child fields within child groups (if any).
      Returns:
      a list of all child fields represented as instances of AbstractNField.

    • equals

      public boolean equals(StringValue value)
      Compares the provided StringValue with the value of the current instance to determine if they are equal. The method uses the string representation of the provided StringValue for comparison. Comparison is case-sensitive and treats null values as not equal.
      Parameters:
      value - the StringValue to be compared with the current instance. A null value will result in the comparison returning false.
      Returns:
      true if the string representation of the provided StringValue is equal to the string representation of the current instance; false otherwise.

    • greaterThan

      public boolean greaterThan(String value)
      Checks whether the current instance is greater than the given string value. The comparison is performed based on the underlying storage format, converting the input string if necessary according to the EBCDIC or ASCII encoding of the current instance.
      Parameters:
      value - the string value to be compared against the current instance. If the instance uses EBCDIC storage, the input string will be encoded to EBCDIC before comparison; otherwise, it will be represented in bytes using ASCII encoding.
      Returns:
      true if the current instance is greater than the provided string value; false otherwise.

    • greaterThan

      public boolean greaterThan(StringValue value)
      Compares the current instance with the string representation of the given StringValue to determine if the current instance is greater. The comparison is performed based on the underlying string value of the provided StringValue.
      Parameters:
      value - the StringValue to be compared against the current instance. A null value will throw a NullPointerException.
      Returns:
      true if the current instance is greater than the string representation of the provided StringValue, false otherwise.

    • lessThan

      public boolean lessThan(String value)
      Checks if the current instance is less than the given string value. The comparison is performed based on the underlying storage format, converting the input string if necessary according to the EBCDIC or ASCII encoding of the current instance.
      Parameters:
      value - the string value to be compared against the current instance. If the instance uses EBCDIC storage, the input string will be encoded to EBCDIC before comparison; otherwise, it will be represented in bytes using ASCII encoding.
      Returns:
      true if the current instance is less than the provided string value; false otherwise.

    • lessThan

      public boolean lessThan(StringValue value)
      Compares the current instance with the string representation of the given StringValue to determine if the current instance is less. The comparison is performed based on the underlying string value of the provided StringValue.
      Parameters:
      value - the StringValue to be compared against the current instance. A null value will throw a NullPointerException.
      Returns:
      true if the current instance is less than the string representation of the provided StringValue, false otherwise.

    • greaterEqualThan

      public boolean greaterEqualThan(String value)
      Compares the given string value to determine if it is greater than or equal to the value represented by the current object based on the internal comparison logic.
      Parameters:
      value - the string value to compare against
      Returns:
      true if the given value is greater than or equal to the current object's value, false otherwise

      Returns:

    • inRange

      public boolean inRange(String floorValue, String ceilValue)
      Checks if the current value is within the range defined by the specified floor and ceiling values.
      Parameters:
      floorValue - the lower bound value of the range as a String
      ceilValue - the upper bound value of the range as a String
      Returns:
      true if the current value lies within the specified range (inclusive), false otherwise
